title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Spiral Review
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a capability and surface decision matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.
Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
Match reasoning effort and autonomy to task complexity, reversibility, and verification cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Surface Fit · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Surface Fit without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose chat, coding-agent, api, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.

content.text: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Surface Fit, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a capability and surface decision matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: All model surfaces differ only in visual appearance. This fails the Surface Fit contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.

hints: Name the Surface Fit precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: All model surfaces differ only in visual appearance.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a capability and surface decision matrix.

Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Provider Portability trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.

content.text: For Provider Portability, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Provider Portability precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

stimulus.title: Provider Portability case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One provider prompt should be copied unchanged everywhere.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions. Artifact under review: a capability and surface decision matrix.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Provider Portability, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Provider Portability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.

hints: Name the Provider Portability precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt model surface. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One provider prompt should be copied unchanged everywhere.
